beats.narration: Every penetrant job answers two questions from two shelves: how to test, and what to accept. A Level Two never mixes them up.

payload.title: TWO SHELVES
payload.sub: One says how to test. One says what to accept.
payload.panelLabel: PT // THE FLAW YOU ANSWER FOR

beats.narration: How to test comes from the method chain. E1417 for aerospace, E165 for industry, ASME Article Six on Code work — chemicals qualified to AMS 2644.

payload.heading: The method chain: how to test

beats.narration: One drawing note is five process decisions — type, method, developer form, sensitivity. Honor every token, but the method standard never accepts the part.
What to accept flows from the design side. Measure the indication, classify it, then compare to the flowed-down limit — not the flaw beneath.

payload.heading: Classify, then compare to the limit
payload.note: MEASURE → 3:1 RULE → COMPARE TO FLOWED-DOWN CRITERIA

beats.narration: So know which shelf you're on. If no criteria were flowed down, stop and ask. When documents conflict, the contract governs.

payload.sub: Missing the acceptance document is missing a document — not a judgment call.
